verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multi-pass transmembrane protein (6 TM helices) that resides in intracellular compartments: the trans-Golgi network, recycling endosomes, late endosomes, and the phagophore assembly site (pre-autophagosomal structure). It cycles between these endomembrane compartments to deliver lipids for autophagosome biogenesis via its phospholipid scramblase activity. No credible surface proteomics, flow cytometry on intact cells, or surface biotinylation data documents ATG9A at the plasma membrane. Contextual checks: (1) cell_state_induced — no evidence of stress-induced PM display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage with documented PM pool; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — ATG9A is not a lysosomal membrane protein shuttled to PM; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM pool; (5) stable_surface_attachment — it is not secreted. No therapeutic program targets ATG9A on the cell surface. It is an endomembrane-resident protein by all available evidence.
